mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness signals peak cyclical demand, prompting equity markets to price in near-term earnings acceleration and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production confirms broad-based economic momentum, reducing recession risk premiums and triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 150 basis points over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating real GDP growth, corporate profit margins expand faster than valuation multiples contract, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions drive price discovery.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds 3.0 percent annualized, indicating that growth acceleration is already fully priced into equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index level crosses 240.0, inflation expectations trigger a repricing of discount rates and compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL crosses the 240.0 threshold, or the signal exhibits no incremental explanatory power over FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls validates underlying economic expansion, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon without requiring specific threshold crossings.
failure_condition: The predictive rank correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to generate positive alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with expanding employment levels indicate sustained household purchasing power, which mechanically lifts corporate cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-PAYEMS signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL changes.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rebound in industrial production signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, which rapidly improves earnings guidance and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns when UNRATE remains above 6.0 percent, indicating that labor market slack overrides supply-side improvements.
